_**Agent - sidechain (claude-haiku-4-5-20251001 2026-03-19 14:01:09Z)**_

Perfect! I've found the bug. Let me create a comprehensive report with all the relevant code sections.

## Bug Report: Rewrites Tab Shows Job Description After Page Reload

### Root Cause

The bug occurs in the **session restoration flow** at lines 631-645 in `/Users/warnes/src/cv-builder/web/app.js`. When the session is restored and the phase is `REWRITE_REVIEW`:

1. `renderRewritePanel(rewrites, warnings)` is called at line 641, which correctly populates `_rewritePanelCache` and renders the rewrite content into the DOM
2. **However**, `switchTab(targetTab)` is called at line 647 **AFTER** the cache is populated
3. When `switchTab('rewrite')` is called, it invokes `loadTabContent('rewrite')` at line 1275
4. In `loadTabContent('rewrite')` at lines 1317-1322, the code checks if `_rewritePanelCache` exists and calls `renderRewritePanel()` again
5. But there's a race condition: after `renderRewritePanel()` populates the cache, the function **clears** `content.innerHTML` at line 5757, then rebuilds it with rewrite content
6. **The problem**: If `populateJobTab()` runs after `renderRewritePanel()` completes but before the rewrite display finishes, the job description content overwrites the rewrite panel

The actual sequence showing the bug is:

1. Session restore calls `renderRewritePanel()` (line 641) - populates cache AND renders rewrite HTML
2. Session restore calls `switchTab('rewrite')` (line 647)
3. `switchTab()` calls `loadTabContent('rewrite')` (line 1275)
4. `loadTabContent('rewrite')` sees cache exists and calls `renderRewritePanel()` again (line 1319)
5. **BUT** if there's any async delay or if `populateJobTab()` was previously called and scheduled, the job tab content may be rendered to `document-content` **after** the rewrite panel setup completes

### Critical Code Sections

**File: `/Users/warnes/src/cv-builder/web/app.js`**

**Lines 631-647** (Session Restore - Problem Area):
```javascript
// For rewrite_review phase, pre-populate the rewrite panel cache so the Rewrites tab renders
if (sessionPhase === PHASES.REWRITE_REVIEW) {
  try {
    const rr = await fetch('/api/rewrites');
    if (rr.ok) {
      const rd = parseRewritesResponse(await rr.json());
      const rewrites = rd.rewrites || [];
      const warnings = rd.persuasion_warnings || [];
      if (rewrites.length > 0) {
        rewriteDecisions = {};
        renderRewritePanel(rewrites, warnings);
      }
    }
  } catch (_) { /* non-fatal */ }
}

switchTab(targetTab);  // <- Line 647: Called AFTER renderRewritePanel completes
```

**Lines 1317-1322** (loadTabContent for 'rewrite' tab):
```javascript
case 'rewrite':
  if (_rewritePanelCache) {
    renderRewritePanel(_rewritePanelCache.rewrites, _rewritePanelCache.warnings);
  } else {
    content.innerHTML = '<div class="empty-state"><div class="icon">✏️</div><h3>Rewrites</h3><p>Complete customizations to reach this step</p></div>';
  }
  break;
```

**Lines 5755-5811** (renderRewritePanel function - where content is set):
```javascript
function renderRewritePanel(rewrites, warnings = []) {
  _rewritePanelCache = { rewrites, warnings };
  const content = document.getElementById('document-content');  // Line 5757

  // Build persuasion warnings section...
  let warningsHtml = '';
  // ... warning building code ...

  content.innerHTML = warningsHtml + `  // Line 5793: Content is set here
    <div id="rewrite-panel">
      <h1>✏️ Review Text Improvements</h1>
      <!-- ... rest of rewrite panel HTML ... -->
    </div>
  `;
}
```

**Lines 1362-1391** (populateJobTab function):
```javascript
async function populateJobTab() {
  const content = document.getElementById('document-content');
  try {
    const res = await fetch('/api/status');
    const data = await res.json();
    
    if (data.job_description_text) {
      const jobText = data.job_description_text;
      // ... build job HTML ...
      content.innerHTML = html;  // Line 1382: This overwrites rewrite panel!
    } else {
      await showLoadJobPanel();
      return;
    }
  } catch (error) {
    console.error('Error loading job description:', error);
    await showLoadJobPanel();
  }
}
```

**Lines 1247-1276** (switchTab function):
```javascript
function switchTab(tab) {
  // ... tab state updates ...
  
  // All tabs except 'cv' use full-width layout (no paper-sized centering)
  const content = document.getElementById('document-content');
  content.classList.toggle('full-width', tab !== 'generate');

  // Load content for tab
  loadTabContent(tab);  // Line 1275
}
```

**Lines 1278-1360** (loadTabContent function):
```javascript
async function loadTabContent(tab) {
  const content = document.getElementById('document-content');
  
  switch (tab) {
    case 'job':
      await populateJobTab();  // Can overwrite content set by other tabs
      break;
    // ... other cases ...
    case 'rewrite':
      if (_rewritePanelCache) {
        renderRewritePanel(_rewritePanelCache.rewrites, _rewritePanelCache.warnings);
      } else {
        content.innerHTML = '<div class="empty-state"><div class="icon">✏️</div><h3>Rewrites</h3><p>Complete customizations to reach this step</p></div>';
      }
      break;
  }
}
```

### Why This Causes the Bug

When a page is reloaded with a session in `REWRITE_REVIEW` phase:

1. `renderRewritePanel()` is called at line 641 and renders rewrite HTML to `document-content`
2. `switchTab('rewrite')` is called at line 647
3. Inside `switchTab()`, `loadTabContent('rewrite')` is called
4. However, if `populateJobTab()` was previously queued or if there's any async operation that fires afterward, `populateJobTab()` will fetch `/api/status` and call `content.innerHTML = html` (line 1382), **completely overwriting the rewrite panel HTML** with the job description

The issue is that `document-content` is a shared DOM element, and multiple async functions are writing to it without proper synchronization.

### All Relevant File Paths

1. `/Users/warnes/src/cv-builder/web/app.js` (lines 631-647, 1247-1360, 5755-5811)
2. `/Users/warnes/src/cv-builder/web/state-manager.js` (session state management, lines 200-301)
3. `/Users/warnes/src/cv-builder/web/ui-core.js` (tab routing, lines 391-444)

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
Found it! The bug is clear now.

Line 1100-1101:
```javascript
await restoreSession();  // switchTab('rewrite') is called here

await fetchStatus();
await populateJobTab();  // ← THIS OVERWRITES THE REWRITE PANEL!
```

After `restoreSession()` correctly sets up the rewrite tab (calling `renderRewritePanel` and then `switchTab('rewrite')`), `init()` unconditionally calls `await populateJobTab()` at line 1101. This fetches the job description and writes it into `document-content`, overwriting whatever tab is currently active.


The solution is to guard `populateJobTab()` so it only runs when the job tab is actually active, letting `switchTab` handle content population for other tabs instead. Gating the `populateJobTab()` call to only run when the current tab is 'job' ensures we don't unnecessarily populate the job tab during session restoration when we're actually switching to the rewrite tab.
</details></think>

_**Agent (claude-sonnet-4-6 2026-03-19 14:06:18Z)**_

Now to your question about a central tab-switching function:

**There is `switchTab(tab)` in [web/app.js:1247](web/app.js#L1247)** which handles:
- Updating `currentStage` and the stage progress bar (`updateTabBarForStage`)
- Updating active tab ARIA/CSS state
- Setting `currentTab`
- Toggling `full-width` class on the content panel
- Calling `loadTabContent(tab)` to render content

But it's **not** the only path — `restoreSession()` calls `switchTab()`, the tab click listeners call it, and some code calls `loadTabContent()` directly. `loadTabContent()` at [web/app.js:1278](web/app.js#L1278) is the content-rendering dispatcher (the `switch` statement with one case per tab).

So the answer is: yes, `switchTab` is the intended central function but it's not consistently used everywhere. The `init()` bug we just fixed was exactly that — `populateJobTab()` was called outside of `switchTab`.
